stats_fallback.gt_note: Use 139,072 from the MSC official Technical Sheet. Wikipedia and some secondary sources cite 139,400 — that figure is outdated/incorrect.
stats_fallback.builder: STX France (now Chantiers de l'Atlantique), Saint-Nazaire
stats_fallback.builder_note: STX France is period-accurate for 2013 delivery; the yard only reverted to Chantiers de l'Atlantique in 2018.
stats_fallback.yacht_club_status: Installed from 2013 delivery (Fantasia class had Yacht Club from build). Forward enclave on Decks 15/16/18: 69 suites, Top Sail Lounge (Deck 15), Moroccan-themed La Palmeraie Restaurant (Deck 15, 110 seats — distinct from sisters' L'Olivo), The One Pool and The One Bar (Deck 18), private concierge reception, library, 24-hour butler. Yacht Club Restaurant refresh scheduled for February 2028.

related.class_notes: Fantasia Class. MSC Preziosa is the fourth and final Fantasia-class ship. She and MSC Divina form the 'modified' Fantasia sub-group at 139,072 GT with 1,751 cabins and two extra elevators (vs Fantasia/Splendida at ~137,936 GT and 1,637 cabins). Each sister uses different MDR and theatre names: Preziosa's are L'Arabesque Restaurant, The Golden Lobster Restaurant, and the Platinum Theatre. Preziosa's Yacht Club restaurant is the Moroccan-themed La Palmeraie — NOT L'Olivo (which is on Fantasia and Splendida).

deployment.spring_2026_reposition: Mid-Atlantic transatlantic crossing from Maceió, Brazil to Funchal, Portugal. Departed Maceió 7 April 2026; current AIS position April 11, 2026 in the North Atlantic (~10.92°N / -24.80°W), 16.9 kn at 24-25° course; ETA Funchal 14 April 2026.
deployment.summer_2026: Hamburg, Germany homeport — 5-, 7-, 11-, and 14-night Northern Europe roundtrips covering Norwegian Fjords, Iceland, British Isles, and North Cape. Secondary embarkation from Southampton, Rotterdam, Zeebrugge, and Le Havre. Sample itineraries: 14-night Hamburg roundtrip 9 August 2026; 11-night Iceland loops 11 June and 23 August 2026.
deployment.winter_2026_27: Western Europe from Hamburg — rotation change. Previous winters saw her in Santos, Brazil; this winter she remains in Europe.

history.contract_history: Hull originally contracted in 2010 by Libya's GNMTC for Hannibal Gaddafi, planned to be named Phoenicia. STX France cancelled the contract during the 2011 Libyan Civil War. MSC Cruises acquired the hull in March 2012 for approximately €550 million and completed construction as the fourth Fantasia-class ship.
history.refits: April 2025 drydock at Damen Shiprepair Rotterdam (~2 weeks): flooring, carpets, upholstery replacement; marine growth removal; thruster/stabilizer/propeller inspections; routine class-related work. Resumed service 13 April 2025 from Hamburg. Scheduled: Yacht Club Restaurant refresh February 2028.
history.incidents: 6 March 2019: passengers hospitalized on connecting flight from Barbados with chest-infection symptoms. April-May 2020: two crew members tested positive for COVID-19 during pandemic pause; 19 Cuban crew later repatriated via another vessel. November 2023: assisted in rescue of 6 fishermen near Ilha Grande, Brazil. January 2024: man-overboard incident reported during New Year's Eve cruise. No major collisions, fires, or quarantines.

discrepancies_flagged.gt: 139,072 (MSC official Technical Sheet, VesselFinder, CruiseMapper, MyShipTracking, CruiseBooking, ShipCruises — 6 sources agree) vs 139,400 (Wikipedia and some secondary sources). Use 139,072.
discrepancies_flagged.passenger_capacity: 3,502 double / 4,345 max (MSC Technical Sheet) vs 3,959 (CruiseBooking alternate counting). Use 3,502 double / 4,345 max.
discrepancies_flagged.builder_name: STX France (period-accurate for 2013 build) vs Chantiers de l'Atlantique (anachronistic — name only restored in 2018). Use STX France.
discrepancies_flagged.yacht_club_restaurant: La Palmeraie on MSC Preziosa and Divina vs L'Olivo on MSC Fantasia and Splendida. Third-party sites frequently conflate these. MSC Preziosa uses La Palmeraie (confirmed from Technical Sheet and ship map PDF).
discrepancies_flagged.kaito_sushi_bar: Not a separate venue on MSC Preziosa. Sushi is served at the Galaxy Restaurant on Deck 16 Acquamarina. Previous venues-v2.json entries listing kaito-sushi-bar for Preziosa were incorrect and have been removed.
discrepancies_flagged.eataly: Not present on MSC Preziosa despite what some older articles may suggest. Not on current ship map PDF or Technical Sheet.
